title: CBAM — Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
summary: EU's carbon pricing tool for imported goods. Prevents carbon leakage by putting a carbon price on imports from outside the EU.
keyPoints: Applies to: Steel, Cement, Aluminium, Fertilizers, Electricity, Hydrogen
Transitional phase: Oct 2023 – Dec 2025 (reporting only)
Full implementation: Jan 2026 (CBAM certificates required)
Importers must declare embedded emissions and surrender CBAM certificates
Price linked to EU ETS auction price

title: GHG Protocol — Greenhouse Gas Accounting
summary: The world's most widely used greenhouse gas accounting standard for companies and cities.
keyPoints: Scope 1: Direct emissions from owned/controlled sources
Scope 2: Indirect emissions from purchased energy
Scope 3: All other indirect emissions in value chain (15 categories)
Corporate Standard, Project Protocol, and Scope 3 Standard are key documents
Basis for most national and corporate reporting frameworks

title: ISO 14064 — GHG Verification Standard
summary: International standard for quantification, monitoring, reporting and verification of GHG emissions.
keyPoints: ISO 14064-1: Org-level GHG inventory specification
ISO 14064-2: Project-level GHG emission reductions
ISO 14064-3: Validation and verification of GHG statements
ISO 14065: Requirements for GHG validation/verification bodies
Used for third-party assurance of corporate GHG reports

title: BRSR — Business Responsibility & Sustainability Reporting
summary: SEBI-mandated ESG disclosure framework for listed Indian companies. Replaced the earlier BRR format.
keyPoints: Mandatory for top 1000 listed companies by market cap
BRSR Core: 49 KPIs with 3rd party assurance for top 150 companies
Covers 9 National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC)
Sections: General Disclosures, Management & Process Disclosures, Principle-wise Performance
Aligns with GRI, SASB and TCFD frameworks

title: Carbon Credits & Carbon Markets
summary: Understanding compliance and voluntary carbon markets, credit types, and trading mechanisms.
keyPoints: Compliance markets: EU ETS, UK ETS, India CCTS, California Cap-and-Trade
Voluntary markets: Verra (VCS), Gold Standard, American Carbon Registry
1 carbon credit = 1 tonne CO2 equivalent avoided or removed
Credit types: Avoidance, Reduction, Removal (highest quality)
Article 6 of Paris Agreement governs international carbon trading

title: Net Zero & Science Based Targets (SBTi)
summary: Corporate net-zero strategies aligned with 1.5°C pathways and SBTi validation.
keyPoints: SBTi Net-Zero Standard requires 90-95% absolute emission reduction
Remaining emissions must be neutralized with permanent removals
Near-term targets: 5-10 year; Long-term: by 2050
Sector-specific pathways: Steel, Cement, Aviation, Finance
Do not confuse with carbon neutrality (offsets-based) claims
